2605.22672.title: Is Capability a Liability? More Capable Language Models Make Worse Forecasts When It Matters Most
2605.22672.paper_summary: We document inverse scaling in LLMs on forecasting problems whose underlying time series exhibit superlinear growth and tail risk of regime change, a structure common in finance and epidemiology. On these tasks, more capable models produce worse distributional forecasts. The pattern appears on ForecastBench-Sim (FBSim), a contamination-free, simulated-world benchmark we release, in forecasting synthetic SIR epidemics with a matched linear control, and replicates in real-world datasets on COVID-19, measles, housing markets, and hyperinflation. A per-quantile decomposition shows the failure concentrates at the upper tail, which more capable models shift upward to track aggressive extrapolations of growth, while the lower tail stays put. A within-family study of Llama-3.1 shows that both model scale and post-training independently contribute to this effect. Domain knowledge does not reliably rescue calibration. This inverse scaling does not appear on single-threshold metrics common in LLM forecasting benchmarks, reversing the sign of the capability--accuracy relationship on identical outputs. Single-threshold scoring at conventional cutoffs misses the upper-tail cost; tail-inclusive scoring reverses the sign of the capability--accuracy relationship on the same outputs. We recommend that LLM forecasting evaluations use continuous (and unbounded) measures of accuracy alongside bounded binary threshold metrics.
2605.22672.paper_summary_zh: 我們記錄了在預測問題中大型語言模型（LLMs）的逆縮放，這些問題的基礎時間序列顯示出超線性增長和政權變更的尾部風險，這種結構在金融和流行病學中很常見。在這些任務中，更強大的模型產生了更差的分佈預測。這一模式出現在我們發布的無污染模擬世界基準ForecastBench-Sim (FBSim)上，該基準在預測合成SIR流行病時，與匹配的線性控制進行比較，並在COVID-19、麻疹、房地產市場和惡性通貨膨脹的真實世界數據集中得到了重現。每個分位數的分解顯示，失敗集中在上尾，更強大的模型將其向上移動，以追蹤增長的激進外推，而下尾則保持不變。對Llama-3.1的家族內研究顯示，模型規模和後訓練獨立地對這一效應有所貢獻。領域知識並不能可靠地改善校準。在大型語言模型預測基準中，這種逆縮放並未出現在單一閾值指標上，反轉了相同輸出上的能力與準確性之間的關係符號。在傳統的截止點進行的單一閾值評分忽略了上尾成本；包含尾部的評分則反轉了相同輸出上的能力與準確性之間的關係符號。我們建議大型語言模型的預測評估使用連續（且無界）準確性度量，並與有界的二元閾值指標一起使用。
2605.22672.author: Nick Merrill et.al.
2605.22672.authors: Nick Merrill, Jaeho Lee, Ezra Karger
